purpose: Receipt-backed research seeds from terse operator notes for the Telos science and analytical-engine lane.

source_receipts.title: Neil Turok official University of Edinburgh profile

claims.claim: Neil Turok is a current research seed for cosmology, theoretical physics, and quantum-gravity-adjacent inquiry.

claim_boundaries: Do not infer endorsement, collaboration, or Telos usage from this seed.
Do not summarize specific Turok papers without resolving the paper metadata and lawful source receipts for that paper.
Treat speculative cosmology as research context, not as verified engineering law.
next_actions: gather.openalex or gather.crossref: resolve current Turok works and citations.
gather.arxiv: find lawful preprint metadata for Turok-related cosmology papers.
crucible.assess: separate falsifiable cosmology claims from analogy or design inspiration.

source_receipts.title: NIST CODATA value for the Planck constant

claims.claim: The SI defines the Planck constant exactly.

claim_boundaries: The exact SI value is a metrology anchor, not a license to treat model outputs as physical measurements.
Derived uses such as h-bar, Planck units, or simulation constants need their own unit conversion receipts.
Scientific or engineering claims using this constant must carry units, dimensional analysis, and source ids.
next_actions: index.context: attach this constant to measurement and unit-conversion context packs.
crucible.assess: check any simulator constant against SI units and dimensional analysis.
telos.action.receipt: preserve exact constants and unit conversions in later model or simulation actions.

seeds.operator_note: bundle theory - differential geometry from bundle perspective

source_receipts.title: Principal bundles and connections modelled by Lie group bundles
A constructive approach to generalized principal connections
Discrete connections on principal bundles: the Discrete Atiyah Sequence

claims.claim: Bundle-theoretic differential geometry is a lawful research lane for modeling local-to-global structure, connections, and gauge-style transport in Telos simulations.
Discrete connection work is relevant when Telos needs replayable, stepwise geometry states rather than only smooth-manifold descriptions.

claim_boundaries: Do not treat bundle terminology as a rendering, verification, or model-state implementation by itself.
Every geometric object promoted into Telos must carry explicit coordinates, charts/trivializations, transition data, units where relevant, and replay receipts.
Gauge-theory analogies require separate source receipts before they are used as engineering claims.
next_actions: gather.arxiv: keep resolving current bundle, connection, and discrete-geometry preprints before implementation work.
index.context: map Telos render-nd, viable-viz, and measurement-overlay modules against local-to-global geometry seams.
crucible.assess: build negative fixtures for broken transition data, unjoinable chart ids, stale connection state, and unverifiable curvature claims.

source_leads.notes: Treat as a local design phrase until a mathematical, graphics, or signal-processing source is resolved.

claims.claim: Zenzic is a historical mathematical term for a square; stacked zenzic forms name repeated powers such as fourth and eighth power.
For Telos, the useful engineering interpretation is a receipt-chained repeated-squaring operation: x, x^2, x^4, x^8, with each intermediate state hashable and replayable.

claim_boundaries: The historical term is evidence-backed; any Telos-specific zenzic operator is an implementation design that must carry its own tests.
Do not promote 'flicker in zenzic operations' as a standard term until Gather resolves a lawful source.
Repeated squaring can compress arithmetic context, but it is not lossless context compression for arbitrary code or documents.
next_actions: forum.route: route 'flicker' to math, graphics, signal-processing, or local-design vocabulary before implementation.
crucible.assess: test zenzic chains with changed intermediate value, missing hash, stale modulus, and unjoinable operation id failures.
telos.context.envelope: evaluate zenzic chains as compact operation receipts for large-context mathematical and rendering work.
